CheckTitle: AI Agent Credentials Must Not Exceed 365 Days of Age
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Identity**. Checks that the age of credentials used by the AI agent does not exceed 365 days. Credentials that have been in use for over a year represent an elevated risk because they have had a prolonged exposure window and may have been unknowingly compromised.
Risk: Long-lived credentials that are never rotated or replaced accumulate risk over time. A credential in use for more than a year may have been inadvertently exposed through logging, debug output, version control, or insider threats — with the breach going undetected.

Code.Other:
1. Immediately rotate any credential that has exceeded 365 days of age.
2. Implement automated rotation using AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, or GCP Secret Manager.
3. Set a maximum credential lifetime policy and enforce it via CI/CD compliance gates.
4. Migrate to short-lived credentials via OIDC Workload Identity to eliminate maximum-age concerns.

Recommendation.Text: Rotate all agent credentials immediately if they exceed 365 days of age. Establish automated rotation with a maximum interval of 90 days, or migrate to OIDC Workload Identity for credential-free authentication.

Notes: ASPM-007 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
